pass_2.diagnosis: The STAND DOWN itself is CORRECT, not a bug -- Stage 10's own language: a ghost refusal is the architecture working. Every regime independently showing the identical shape (good train WR, holdout collapse of -19 to -44pp, enh-check correctly detecting overfit, correctly falling back to equal weights) is not 9 separate failures -- it is the SAME underlying methodology issue expressed 9 times. The `Total combos: 562500` figure is the real lead: searching 562,500 weight combinations against a single train/holdout split, with no multiple-testing correction sized to that count and no walk-forward across multiple non-overlapping windows (SS-III.6's own requirement), is close to a textbook overfitting generator. The existing enh-check/fit-check safety nets are correctly catching the SYMPTOM every time (hence equal-weight fallback, hence the correct STAND DOWN) -- but the search itself doesn't know how much fishing it did.

pass_3.diagnosis: OptimizeSessionWeightsPerRegime (Phase 3B.5, ~line 5894) gated its coordinate-descent weight fit at `sCnt < 20` and accepted the fitted result over the regime-level prior whenever `finalTrades>=30 && WR_delta>=2.0pp` -- both drastically below the n>=500 floor SS-III.4 makes binding ('a cell with n<500 may not update a weight, ever, on any evidence'). pass_5.operational_note: IMPORTANT TOOLING FINDING: MQL4/Logs/<date>.log (the standard MT4 'Experts' Journal tab file) is severely buffered during heavy calibration compute -- observed stale for 9+ minutes at a time while real progress was happening. The EA maintains its OWN dedicated real-time log at MQL4/Files/TUNGSTEN_live.log for exactly this reason -- but that file is OVERWRITTEN (not appended) on every update, holding only the single most-recent line. A live `tail -f` monitor catches every update as it happens, but the file CANNOT be retroactively grepped for history once overwritten -- the monitor stream itself is the only durable record of what happened mid-run. Future passes needing live calibration visibility should watch MQL4/Files/TUNGSTEN_live.log via a persistent streaming monitor, not the MQL4/Logs Journal file.
pass_5.diagnosis: Observed live: every one of the 9 regimes' Phase 3B search independently concluded 'no edge found -> equal weights, thr 70' (consistent with the real pass-2 STAND DOWN calibration's own pattern -- two independent runs reaching the same 'no real edge here' conclusion, a good consistency signal for the discipline working correctly, not a bug). This drove the ghost gate into its P1-BASELINE RESCUE SCAN path (fires when OOS qualifying trades <20), which the source (~line 9515-9550) showed tests exactly ONE fixed threshold -- g_BaseThreshold, i.e. the same 70 that Phase 3B's own per-regime search had just found no edge at -- rather than searching the gap between typical per-regime-tuned thresholds (28-55, edge-less) and the baseline (70, also just found edge-less in this run). Manuel's live question ('why are thresholds fixed and stationary during testing') was pointing at exactly this real, confirmed gap.
pass_5.action_taken: WARRANT#15: widened the P1-baseline rescue scan from a single fixed-threshold check to a bounded 5-candidate sweep {50,55,60,65,70}, scoring each candidate by WR*sqrt(n) and keeping whichever clears the SAME pre-existing acceptance bar (>=15 OOS trades, >=42% WR) with the best composite score. The acceptance bar itself was NOT loosened -- only the search breadth widened from 1 point to 5. Applied and compiled while the live SLS(32) calibration continued running unaffected in a separate process (editing/recompiling source never touches an already-loaded .ex4 in a running terminal). Version bumped 9.46->9.47, EA_BUILD_ID SLS(32)->SLS(33).
pass_5.deferred_not_fixed: Explicitly NOT attempting a formal multiple-testing correction for the widened 5-candidate sweep -- the pre-existing 42% WR / 15-trade bar was already a fixed engineering heuristic, not a powered statistical test, so bolting on a Bonferroni-style correction here would be false rigor rather than real rigor. This is a real, honestly-flagged, bounded risk: testing 5 thresholds instead of 1 modestly raises the chance of a false-positive 'viable' rescue purely by chance. Recommend watching, in future calibrations, whether this rescue scan starts reporting 'viable' meaningfully more often than the single-threshold version did historically -- if so, that is evidence of the exposure materialising and should trigger a proper correction (same class of concern as hypothesis #3, still open). did_this_pass_improve_the_agent_pass_4.honest_summary: This pass did exactly what it was asked: another real, bounded, mechanical polishing pass, fully finished (compiled clean, ledger closed, not left mid-task). It also validated Lesson#5's own hypothesis about itself -- that the defect class it named was systemic, not isolated -- by finding 3 more real instances the moment a systematic search was actually run instead of merely recommended. The project's structural discipline (every gate now provably at n>=500 where WARRANT#9's class applies, across 4 passes and 6 independently-discovered instances) is real and getting more thorough. But the honest headline across all 4 passes remains unchanged: nothing has been measured against a real calibration or live run yet. Continuing to polish without ever closing that loop risks repeating, at the meta level, exactly the failure mode the whole tungsten-forge project exists to prevent in TUNGSTEN itself -- activity that FEELS like progress but has never been checked against reality. That is not a reason to have refused this pass (Manuel explicitly asked for it, and static discipline work is genuinely valuable on its own), but it is the one thing that should not keep being deferred indefinitely.

pass_8.diagnosis:
Dense, exhaustive cross-check of the full EA<->indicator interface, four dimensions, all 9 companion indicators (ART, ATM, CAN, ETF, KTF, PRL, SPG, SQE, VRC):
(1) PARAMETER FIDELITY -- every one of the 51 iCustom() call sites (2 near-duplicate blocks: IE_Update() at ~line 11064-11183 for live shift=1, IE_ReadAtBar(sh,...) at ~line 11189-11284 for calibration) was matched value-for-value, in order, against each indicator's current `input` block. All 9 indicators: 100% match, zero drift, including the 3 indicators pass 6/7 touched (ATM's offset-clamp fix, VRC's composite-ratio fix, SPG's session-window fix) -- none of those edits perturbed the indicators' own input signatures, so the EA's hardcoded call-site argument lists (which override the indicator's own compiled-in defaults regardless, per MQL4 iCustom semantics) remain correct.
(2) BUFFER SEMANTICS -- cross-checked every buffer index the EA reads (via grep on SetIndexBuffer in each indicator source) against the EA's own inline comments claiming what that buffer means (e.g. 'VRC buf1=composite vol ratio', 'SPG buf1=vwap buf4=inSession', 'ATM buf0/1=fast/slow EMA buf4/5=arrow buf6=cloud strength', 'SQE buf6=CompositeScore', 'CAN buf5=SigScore', 'KTF buf5=variance/trust', 'PRL buf8=BiasScore', 'ART buf5=OuterDist'). All verified exact matches across all 9 indicators -- zero buffer-index drift.
(3) CALIBRATION/LIVE PARITY (Part V) at the indicator-read layer -- line-by-line diff of IE_Update() (live, hardcoded shift=1/2) against IE_ReadAtBar(sh,...) (calibration, parameterized): identical transformation logic for all 9 indicators, INCLUDING WARRANT#19's VRC amplitude fix and WARRANT#21's SPG_SessionWindow() call -- both are correctly mirrored in both code paths, not just the live one. This is the single most important finding of the pass: it directly confirms two of pass 7's warrants did not silently diverge between the two paths, which is exactly the failure mode Part V exists to catch.
(4) SCALE/DEGENERACY sweep -- grepped all 9 indicator .mq4 files for Point*N: zero remaining illegal-absolute sites (WARRANT#17's ATM fix, from pass 6, is the only historical instance and is still correctly in place as atr14-ratio code, unchanged). Structural parity (brace/paren balance) verified balanced on all 9 files. Recompiled all 9 unchanged: still 0 errors/0 warnings each, matching the pass-6 baseline exactly.
One apparent anomaly was investigated and RULED OUT as a false lead: ComputeEntryConviction() (line ~37195, a live-only pre-execution gate using TimeCurrent()/Time[0] throughout, never invoked from calibration/ghost code) calls IE_Update() (which internally uses MT4 shift=1, the last closed bar) but then calls IE_KTFTrust(0) with a literal shift of 0. This looked like an internal shift-convention mismatch at first read. Checked against GetIndicatorContribution()'s own explicit, documented convention (line ~11417: 'if(sh==0) // LIVE: use the live ensemble snapshot') -- sh=0 is an established codebase-wide sentinel meaning 'live/current', distinct from a literal MT4 bar-shift argument, used consistently elsewhere in this exact subsystem. IE_KTFTrust(0) is correctly using that same convention, not violating it. No fix filed -- confirmed as correct after checking the precedent, not assumed.

This is a deliberate, reported outcome (see scope_decision), not an omission: the pass did the dense cross-check Manuel asked for and it came back clean. CATREC's regime-preservation gate (line ~46335, drifted from pass 7's ~46322 reference due to intervening comment-only additions, logic byte-identical: `trades<5 -> preserveRegimes=0.3`) was re-located and reconfirmed untouched, exactly as pass 7 left it pending Manuel's judgment call on intent -- not touched this pass either.

This happened during SLS(43)'s Phase 3B regime 2 (Strong Bear) global-random search, around draw 30000/50000 per TUNGSTEN_live.log's last write. An Application Hang (Windows detected the process's message loop stopped responding) is a DIFFERENT class of event from an Application Error (crash/exception) or a silent process exit -- both of which were checked and ruled out in prior passes without ever finding this. Checked +-4 minutes around the hang for coincident System-log activity (RestartManager, VSS, Kernel-Power): NONE found this time, unlike the previous session's (unconfirmed, correlational) Windows-Update-adjacent finding. Current free memory post-crash was ~31% (2.59GB/8.1GB) -- not obviously critical, though this is AFTER terminal.exe's own memory was released by the OS killing it, so it doesn't rule out memory pressure having been the trigger. LEADING HYPOTHESIS: Phase 3B's 50,000-draw global-random search (and similar long, synchronous, tight-loop grid searches elsewhere in the calibration path) runs long enough between yields that Windows' hang-detector (default ~5s of no message-pump activity) flags and kills the process, even though the EA is still genuinely working, not deadlocked. pass_36.diagnosis: Full log review (not just the abbreviated live-log lines the Monitor captured) found the real root cause chain: (1) Phase 1 itself is barely above random (30.0% WR vs ~29% floor, explicit self-diagnosis 'components not discriminative') -- the 6 base CEUS components carry almost no raw directional signal on this calibration window (66056 M5 bars, ~91% training/~9% OOS). (2) Phase 3B's search (562500 weight combinations tested per the EA's own log) runs against a SINGLE train/holdout split with no multiple-testing correction and no walk-forward across independent windows -- exactly the methodology gap flagged as 'hypothesis #3, DEFERRED' back in the SLS(29)/(30) session (tungsten_forge_sls30_session memory) and never fixed since. With near-zero real signal, this guarantees every regime's chosen combo fits the split's own noise and fails holdout, identically, every time. (3) The internal retry loop ('attempt 1/3') only widens score thresholds, not the underlying components -- solving the wrong variable, which is why retries don't help. (4) Tonight's own new Pre-Flight data independently corroborates weak/unstable signal: every day-of-week cell TRANSITIONING (sign flips within only 5 sub-windows) and 8 cells flagged SIGN-INVERSION with strong t-stats. (5) Traced whether this reflects an actual sign-convention BUG: compared Living-Edge's fwd=(Close[b-LE_HORIZON]-Close[b])/atr (positive=price rose=long-favourable) against Phase 3B's own barOutcomes.longPnL convention (also positive=price rose from entry=long-favourable) -- CONSISTENT, no cross-system sign bug found. (6) Found instead a concrete, confirmed bug in Gate 9's Pre-Flight PRINT: it only ever tested t<-3.0, never t>+3.0 (zero positive-tail or |t| checks anywhere in the file) -- meaning the diagnostic report silently discarded roughly half the statistically significant cells, making the situation look more one-sidedly 'inverted' than the real data supports. Confirmed the LIVE decision this gate informs (WARRANT#30's ComputeLivingEdgeInversionTrim) was ALWAYS correctly symmetric, so this asymmetry never affected an actual trade -- diagnostic-completeness bug only, not a live-trading bug. (7) Found a gap introduced this session: WARRANT#34/35/39's relationship-measurement machinery ran silently during tonight's calibration (hooked into MeasureRegimeIC) with zero logging anywhere -- its results were lost the moment the process was killed.
